{"society_power": 6.5, "state_power": 8.0, "society_change": -0.5, "state_change": -0.5, "key_event": "Red Scare, labor decline; suffrage partly offsets", "reasoning": "The First Red Scare, defeated 1919 strike wave, collapsing unions, and immigration restriction suppressed key pillars of civil society, though women's suffrage and dense associational life cushioned the decline. State power eased slightly as wartime mobilization wound down, even as administrative capacity (Budget Act, Prohibition enforcement) persisted amid exposed enforcement limits and scandals like Teapot Dome."}
